title: The Spiders Episode

jokes.text: No, no, no, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Warm-up host tells audience the flashing sign says 'applesauce'
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction that it actually says 'applause' - the obvious thing
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection gag that establishes Hank's slightly awkward warm-up energy. Gets the job done but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, no, no, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'

jokes.text: And now, because we've tried it with just the desk... and it really isn't the same... Larry Sanders!
jokes.setup: Standard late-night show introduction
jokes.punchline: Hank breaks format to acknowledge they've experimented with different show openings
jokes.explanation: Nice meta joke about the show's production process. Character-specific for Hank and shows the behind-the-scenes reality bleeding into the performance.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And now, because we've tried it with just the desk... and it really isn't the same... Larry Sanders!

jokes.text: My dog ran by and burst into flames... and rolled to put himself out 'cause he read the pamphlets... they pass out to dogs.
jokes.setup: It was 110 degrees in the Valley
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurdity: dog bursts into flames, then consciously follows fire safety protocol
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist escalation with the unexpected detail about pamphlets for dogs. Well-structured with good commitment to the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My dog ran by and burst into flames... and rolled to put himself out 'cause he read the pamphlets... they pass out to dogs.

jokes.text: because who wants to be in a cast for six weeks? And then, you know, everybody wants to sign it. It's embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Talking about vasectomies
jokes.punchline: Treating vasectomy like a broken limb with a cast that people sign
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic mixing two unrelated medical concepts. The 'signing the cast' detail sells the whole premise beautifully.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: because who wants to be in a cast for six weeks? And then, you know, everybody wants to sign it. It's embarrassing.

jokes.text: The next option is a bad haircut.
jokes.setup: Vasectomy lowers risk of transmitting sexual diseases
jokes.punchline: Suggesting bad haircuts as alternative birth control/STD prevention
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum funny per word. The deadpan delivery and absurd logic work well together.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The next option is a bad haircut.

jokes.text: but was still so embarrassed he also bought... an $8,000 toothpaste tube... and $5,000 pack of razor blades.
jokes.setup: Man bought 150-year-old condom at auction for $6,300
jokes.punchline: Escalating embarrassment leading to purchasing overpriced toiletries as cover
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific dollar amounts making the absurdity more concrete. Relatable embarrassment taken to logical extreme.

dialogue.line: but was still so embarrassed he also bought... an $8,000 toothpaste tube... and $5,000 pack of razor blades.

jokes.text: This is actually the same outfit you wear to drive in L.A.
jokes.setup: Larry is wearing full hockey gear
jokes.punchline: Comparing hockey protection to what you need driving in LA
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about LA driving. Nothing groundbreaking but well-executed and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is actually the same outfit you wear to drive in L.A.

jokes.text: He calls it 'the tarantula arm races.'
jokes.setup: Describing the bug man's spider stunt
jokes.punchline: Play on 'arms race' with literal arms and spiders racing up them
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. The pun feels natural rather than forced.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: He calls it 'the tarantula arm races.'

jokes.text: Two minutes ago, you were willing to take a puck to the nuts. Now you're crying over a couple of bugs.
jokes.setup: Larry is hesitant about the spider stunt
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the contradiction between hockey courage and spider fear
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Artie - direct, cutting logic. The callback to the hockey segment adds layers and shows character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Two minutes ago, you were willing to take a puck to the nuts. Now you're crying over a couple of bugs.

jokes.text: Gigantic fuzzy insects crawling on your body, lions crapping in your lap. It's great television.
jokes.setup: Arguing that the spider stunt is a showbiz tradition
jokes.punchline: Escalating from spiders to lions defecating, all in service of 'great television'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - cynical showbiz wisdom delivered with complete conviction. The escalation from bugs to lion excrement is wonderfully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Gigantic fuzzy insects crawling on your body, lions crapping in your lap. It's great television.

jokes.text: build like this big Santa Fe style Spanish adobe house there. Then you'll be jealous because you can't live there, right?
jokes.setup: Hank spiraling about what spiders might do to Larry
jokes.punchline: Spiders building architectural structures in Larry's head, leading to real estate envy
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Hank - neurotic escalation to absurd extremes. The specificity of 'Santa Fe style Spanish adobe' makes it funnier and very character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: build like this big Santa Fe style Spanish adobe house there. Then you'll be jealous because you can't live there, right?

jokes.text: The night is young. Ow!
jokes.setup: Hank asks if she's had anything big and hairy crawling on her
jokes.punchline: Suggestive response followed by apparent physical pain
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo followed by physical comedy beat. The 'Ow!' suggests immediate consequences for the sexual reference.

dialogue.speaker: Jancey
dialogue.line: The night is young. Ow!

jokes.text: They're just the deadliest spider known to man.
jokes.setup: Telling Larry not to worry about the tarantulas
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting the reassurance with alarming information
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank - trying to be helpful but making things worse. The deadpan delivery of alarming information is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: They're just the deadliest spider known to man.

jokes.text: Watch your step. I mean careerwise.
jokes.setup: Seems like standard polite farewell
jokes.punchline: Clarification reveals it's actually a professional threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Hank passive-aggression. The clarification completely recontextualizes the seemingly innocent warning.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Watch your step. I mean careerwise.

jokes.text: having a comedy club in my own home. Are you saying that 'cause you watered down the drinks?
jokes.setup: Wife complains about having a comedy club at home
jokes.punchline: Larry deflects with accusation about drink strength
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Larry's defensive deflection tactics. Shows marital dynamic effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Jancey
dialogue.line: having a comedy club in my own home.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Are you saying that 'cause you watered down the drinks?

jokes.text: Geez, I hope one of those spiders bites you tomorrow night.
jokes.setup: Frustrated with Larry's comedy obsession
jokes.punchline: Wife expressing genuinely dark wish for minor harm
jokes.explanation: Dark marital humor that feels real. The specific wish shows how fed up she is while staying in comedic territory.

dialogue.speaker: Jancey
dialogue.line: Geez, I hope one of those spiders bites you tomorrow night.

jokes.text: So, can we start a family if I have the baby at the Greek Theater? Huh?
jokes.setup: Larry will only confront fears in front of 20 million people for laughs
jokes.punchline: Proposing public childbirth as equivalent test of his commitment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that perfectly captures the absurdity of Larry's psychology. The Greek Theater specificity makes it funnier and shows deep frustration with his priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Jancey
dialogue.line: So, can we start a family if I have the baby at the Greek Theater? Huh?

jokes.text: But in the shape of a spider, honey.
jokes.setup: Jancey says 'It's a sock' dismissively
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic clarification that the sock's spider shape matters
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's neurosis about even sock-shaped spiders. The earnest clarification makes his fear seem both pathetic and endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: But in the shape of a spider, honey.

jokes.text: Intimacy.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if she's afraid of anything
jokes.punchline: Single word answer revealing deep psychological issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that reveals everything about the character and creates awkward moment. Maximum impact with minimum words.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Intimacy.

jokes.text: Were they Ludes? Soapers? Were they reds?
jokes.setup: Larry desperately wants to know what tranquilizers Beverly uses
jokes.punchline: Escalating through various drug slang terms with increasing desperation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's desperate need for chemical courage. The slang terms show his desperation escalating.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Were they Ludes? Soapers? Were they reds?

jokes.text: Ooh, pubic hair on my coconut. That's it. Sexual harassment. Where is my lawyer?
jokes.setup: Carol and Larry doing Tarzan sketch, Larry mentions pubic hair on coconut
jokes.punchline: Carol immediately escalates to sexual harassment lawsuit
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from innocent prop comedy to major lawsuit. Carol's immediate pivot to legal action is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Ooh, pubic hair on my coconut. That's it. Sexual harassment. Where is my lawyer?

jokes.text: They look like big, hairy potatoes with legs in 'em.
jokes.setup: Larry describing the spiders he saw backstage
jokes.punchline: Specific, vivid description that makes spiders sound both gross and absurd
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific imagery that perfectly captures both the horror and absurdity. The unexpected comparison to potatoes is memorable and visual.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They look like big, hairy potatoes with legs in 'em.

jokes.text: They're roller coasters. Call it what you like.
jokes.setup: Artie calls them 'rolly-coasters'
jokes.punchline: Dismissive correction followed by immediate surrender
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Artie - tries to correct himself then immediately gives up. Shows his gruff pragmatism.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: They're roller coasters. Call it what you like.

jokes.text: I think he's trying out a new Lebanese thing.
jokes.setup: Discussing Larry's bad Tarzan voice
jokes.punchline: Completely random ethnic interpretation of the voice
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist explanation for bad performance. The specificity of 'Lebanese' makes it funnier than generic ethnic reference.

dialogue.speaker: Writer
dialogue.line: I think he's trying out a new Lebanese thing.

jokes.text: You know, talking to you is like talking to you.
jokes.setup: Frustrated with confusing conversation
jokes.punchline: Tautological statement that says nothing while saying everything
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tautology that perfectly captures the frustration of circular conversation. Pure Artie - cutting through BS with absurd directness.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know, talking to you is like talking to you.

jokes.text: Yes, it is. The fastest light in the West. Fire 'em up, partner.
jokes.setup: Artie guesses Hank is doing Quick Light Briquets commercial
jokes.punchline: Hank delivers full commercial pitch with Western catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's commercial desperation and enthusiasm for even minor endorsement work.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yes, it is. The fastest light in the West. Fire 'em up, partner.

jokes.text: Stunts are Larry's territory. Hank Kingsley shalt not trespass.
jokes.setup: Artie offers Hank the spider stunt
jokes.punchline: Hank speaks of himself in third person with mock-biblical language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - formal, pompous language hiding insecurity about show hierarchy. The biblical phrasing is characteristically pretentious.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Stunts are Larry's territory. Hank Kingsley shalt not trespass.

jokes.text: Not that I've made a stink about it, because I am not... a squeaky wheel, you know that.
jokes.setup: Hank claims he's not unhappy about lack of opportunities
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by referencing complaints while denying he complains
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank self-contradiction - denying he complains while actively complaining. The 'squeaky wheel' metaphor is perfectly chosen.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Not that I've made a stink about it, because I am not... a squeaky wheel, you know that.

jokes.text: The hat?
jokes.setup: Artie leaves after successful negotiation
jokes.punchline: Hank notices Artie forgot his hat, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Simple awkward beat. Hank's literal-mindedness about the forgotten hat deflates the dramatic exit moment.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: The hat?

jokes.text: Between you and me, I think I saw your balls at one point.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he looked like an idiot in the loincloth
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt, inappropriate observation about accidental exposure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - inappropriately direct about embarrassing situation. Creates maximum discomfort with casual delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Between you and me, I think I saw your balls at one point.

jokes.text: Did you really see my balls?
jokes.setup: Artie's casual mention of seeing Larry's testicles
jokes.punchline: Larry's mortified follow-up question seeking confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke. Larry's need for confirmation makes the embarrassment worse while staying true to character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Did you really see my balls?

jokes.text: That's my cup from the hockey segment.
jokes.setup: Hank notices something in Larry's office
jokes.punchline: Larry identifies it as his athletic cup, creating awkward display situation
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier hockey segment. The awkwardness of having a jockstrap on display in his office is mildly amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: That's my cup from the hockey segment.

jokes.text: My God... you're a good man.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate lie about giving Hank opportunities
jokes.punchline: Hank's emotional, sincere response to what's actually manipulation
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment - Hank's genuine emotional response to Larry's self-serving lie. The sincerity makes it both funny and uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: My God... you're a good man.

jokes.text: Hank, please.
jokes.setup: Hank becomes emotional about Larry's 'generosity'
jokes.punchline: Larry's uncomfortable attempt to stop the emotional display
jokes.explanation: Good cringe follow-up. Larry's discomfort with Hank's genuine emotion while knowing he's being manipulative creates perfect tension.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Hank, please.

jokes.text: Could you take your top off? I'm begging you. Could you?
jokes.setup: Writers' room with female writer present
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual request from male writer
jokes.explanation: Workplace harassment 'humor' that feels more uncomfortable than funny. Relies on shock value rather than clever writing.

dialogue.line: Could you take your top off? I'm begging you. Could you?

jokes.text: You know, make it more my style.
jokes.setup: Hank asking writers to adjust spider material for him
jokes.punchline: Hank's assumption that he has a distinctive comedic style worth adapting to
jokes.explanation: Good Hank delusion moment. His belief that he has a recognizable comedic style that needs to be accommodated shows his inflated self-perception.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You know, make it more my style.

jokes.text: I carry a spider anti-venom kit on my person at all times. I didn't bring it with me today.
jokes.setup: Expert reassures Hank about spider safety
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he doesn't actually have the safety equipment with him
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction joke. The expert's confidence immediately undermined by practical oversight creates nice tension.

dialogue.speaker: Spider Expert
dialogue.line: I carry a spider anti-venom kit on my person at all times. I didn't bring it with me today.

jokes.text: It's not easy being on this side of the cue cards.
jokes.setup: Artie commenting on difficult decisions
jokes.punchline: Reference to being behind-the-scenes rather than on camera
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about the power dynamics of television. Good character insight for Artie's behind-the-scenes role.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It's not easy being on this side of the cue cards.

jokes.text: Your check is on the way.
jokes.setup: Ear tug signal meant 'Hi, I'm fine'
jokes.punchline: Adding the practical financial reassurance for grandmother
jokes.explanation: Nice addition to the ear tug story. The practical financial element adds humor to what could be purely sentimental.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Your check is on the way.

jokes.text: You actually have to get earmuffs at a specialty shop?
jokes.setup: Carol's earlobe is a quarter inch longer
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd practical concern about specialized winter gear
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist leap from minor physical detail to major practical inconvenience. Shows Larry's tendency to overthink trivial details.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You actually have to get earmuffs at a specialty shop?

jokes.text: maybe you could talk to costumes about getting you a longer loincloth. I saw your balls.
jokes.setup: Carol suggesting they might do the sketch again
jokes.punchline: Blunt revelation that she also witnessed Larry's wardrobe malfunction
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that amplifies Larry's embarrassment. Carol's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier and more mortifying.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: maybe you could talk to costumes about getting you a longer loincloth. I saw your balls.

jokes.text: You're wrong right there. They're clearly found in plastic boxes.
jokes.setup: Spider expert explains tarantulas are from tropical/desert regions
jokes.punchline: Larry's literal-minded observation about their current location
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan literal interpretation. Larry's nervous pedantry comes through as he deflects from his fear with weak observational humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're wrong right there. They're clearly found in plastic boxes.

jokes.text: I've never complained about anyone opening up a lid of anything.
jokes.setup: Spider expert asks permission to open spider container
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly broad statement about his lid-opening policy
jokes.explanation: Good nervous overcompensation from Larry. The unnecessary generalization reveals his anxiety about the specific lid being opened.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I've never complained about anyone opening up a lid of anything.

jokes.text: Wrong. Come on, don't you folks wanna see Hank put that on his...
jokes.setup: Expert suggests putting spider on Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate attempt to deflect to Hank
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's cowardice and willingness to throw Hank under the bus. His desperation is palpable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wrong. Come on, don't you folks wanna see Hank put that on his...

jokes.text: If you guys are too chicken, I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Larry and Hank both avoiding the spider stunt
jokes.punchline: Carol volunteers, making the men look cowardly
jokes.explanation: Great reversal where the guest shows more courage than the hosts. Carol calling them 'chicken' adds to their embarrassment.

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: If you guys are too chicken, I'll do it.

jokes.text: And you were 'mistakener.'
jokes.setup: Carol says she thought Larry was 'yeller'
jokes.punchline: Larry's made-up word following the pattern
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay creating symmetrical exchange. Larry's attempt at clever wordsmithing under pressure is characteristically awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: And you were 'mistakener.'

jokes.text: Don't you drop me!
jokes.setup: Larry panics when spider is placed on his arm
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate plea as he presumably jumps into someone's arms
jokes.explanation: Peak physical comedy moment. Larry's complete panic and loss of dignity creates maximum chaos and embarrassment.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Don't you drop me!

jokes.text: It wasn't a sketch. It was a massive spastic fuck-up.
jokes.setup: Artie tries to frame the spider disaster as a sketch with Carol Burnett
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment of the catastrophe
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment. Larry's brutal honesty about his performance failure is both funny and emotionally real.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It wasn't a sketch. It was a massive spastic fuck-up.

jokes.text: It made the Ed Ames tomahawk throw look like a big piece of shit.
jokes.setup: Comparing Larry's spider disaster to famous TV moments
jokes.punchline: Claiming Larry's disaster makes a legendary Tonight Show moment look bad
jokes.explanation: Excellent insider reference that elevates Larry's disaster to legendary status. Perfect Artie mix of showbiz knowledge and crude assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It made the Ed Ames tomahawk throw look like a big piece of shit.

jokes.text: Oh, no, the spider guy said it's a mild allergic reaction. It should run its course in about a week's time... and it's hardly noticeable.
jokes.setup: Hank apparently has visible spider bite reaction
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's 'hardly noticeable' while clearly being very noticeable
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy payoff. Hank's denial about the obvious visible reaction adds to the absurdity of the spider expert's assessment.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, no, the spider guy said it's a mild allergic reaction. It should run its course in about a week's time... and it's hardly noticeable.

tmdb_overview: Larry battles his reluctance when real live spiders are scheduled for the show, and tries to find a sketch to do with guest Carol Burnett.
